reasoning: Kim Keon Hee was officially arrested on August 12/13, 2025, after a Seoul court issued a pre-trial detention warrant due to charges of bribery, stock manipulation, and election meddling. Under South Korean legal procedures, prosecutors typically have a maximum of 20 days to formally indict a suspect who is in pre-trial detention (a 10-day initial period, plus one 10-day extension). Counting 20 days from August 12/13 places the final deadline for her formal indictment right at the end of August or exactly on September 1. Given the intense momentum of the special counsel's investigation—which began in June and likely faces its own statutory conclusion in September—and the magnitude of the charges, it is virtually certain she will be formally criminally charged (indicted). Additionally, obtaining an arrest warrant itself is predicated on laying out specific criminal charges. The only minor risk denying a YES resolution is if the formal indictment is delayed precisely to September 1 or 2, thereby missing the literal 'before September 1' threshold, but prosecutors usually secure the indictment prior to the absolute last day of detention. Additionally, there are scattered news reports from July 2025 suggesting she might have already been formally indicted on other preliminary charges, reinforcing the overwhelmingly high probability.
